response: ANSWER: Notable large language models mentioned in the context are GPT-3 by Brown et al. (2020), Jurassic by Lieber et al. (2021), Gopher by Rae et al. (2021), MT-NLG 530B by Smith et al. (2022), and Chinchilla (no specific creator cited). These models are dense transformer models that employ various techniques such as pre-training and fine-tuning for natural language generation tasks. Other related works include Retrieval-Augmented Language Models (Feng et al., 2023), LM-Infinite for length generalization (Han et al., 2023), Langchain (Chase, 2022), and Piperag for fast retrieval-augmented generation (Jiang et al., 2024).

5
Discussion
5.1
Implications for alignment research
This research is part of our broader research program to align AI systems with human intentions (Chris-
tiano et al., 2017; Ziegler et al., 2019; Stiennon et al., 2020). Even though this work focuses on
our current language model systems, we seek general and scalable methods that work for future AI
response: ANSWER: The datasets do not explicitly encourage common-sense reasoning and entailment recognition in natural language processing tasks within the given context. Instead, they focus on classification, question answering, summarization, translation, and open-ended generation tasks where evaluation can be done automatically. However, indirectly, these tasks might require some level of common sense and entailment understanding since they involve complex linguistic structures and semantics. Additionally, approaches like learning to feed relevant context through various techniques (e.g., vector search, keyword search, text-to-SQL) may help improve the system's ability to understand and respond appropriately, which implicitly involves recognizing relationships between different pieces of information and making reasonable assumptions based on them.
